description_long: This is a small, single-story wooden cabin with a prominent gable roof covered in reddish-brown shingles. The exterior walls are constructed from light-colored wooden planks, giving it a rustic and natural appearance. The front facade features a simple wooden porch with a short set of steps leading up to a central door, flanked by two sets of multi-pane windows. Both longer sides of the cabin have additional windows, arranged symmetrically, allowing ample natural light inside. The rear of the cabin is plain, with no visible windows or doors, emphasizing its utilitarian design. The structure is elevated slightly above the ground on short supports, suggesting protection from moisture and improved ventilation.
description: A small, rustic wooden cabin with a gable roof, front porch, and multiple symmetrically arranged windows. The exterior is light wood with a reddish-brown shingled roof.
description_view_0: The front view shows the gable roof, central door, porch with steps, and two sets of windows on either side of the entrance.
description_view_1: The left side view displays the long wooden wall with a single multi-pane window positioned centrally.
description_view_2: The rear view reveals a plain wooden wall with no windows or doors, highlighting the cabin's simple back facade.
description_view_3: The right side view mirrors the left, featuring a long wall with two symmetrically placed multi-pane windows.
attribute_roof_type: gable roof with moderate overhangs, covered in reddish-brown asphalt shingles
attribute_window_arrangement: Multiple rectangular multi-pane windows symmetrically arranged: two on the front facade flanking the door, one centrally on the left side, and two symmetrically on the right side
attribute_door_placement: Single exterior door centered on the front facade, accessed via a small porch
